findings.title: CLI flag composition: --no-role-prompts=false silently overrides ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true
findings.summary: In cmd/acr/main.go, RolePromptsSet treats --no-role-prompts as a flag-source signal while RolePrompts is computed as `rolePrompts && !noRolePrompts`. With ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true, passing --no-role-prompts=false flips the env-derived true to false — a precedence design bug.
messages: cmd/acr/main.go の `RolePromptsSet: cmd.Flags().Changed("role-prompts") || cmd.Flags().Changed("no-role-prompts")` と `RolePrompts: rolePrompts && !noRolePrompts` の組み合わせにより、ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true 環境下で --no-role-prompts=false を指定すると、flag が env を override して結果 false になる

findings.title: AutoPhaseDiffPrompt suppresses cross-file findings in single-phase (small/flat) auto-phase runs
findings.summary: resolvePrompts' default branch selects AutoPhaseDiffPrompt even when no arch reviewer exists (small/flat phase). That prompt's "Skip: Cross-file architectural concerns" directive then silently drops cross-file issues — the phase precondition and prompt wording are misaligned.
messages: internal/agent/diff_review.go の resolvePrompts default 分岐が、auto-phase の単一 phase（small/flat 経路）で arch リビュアが存在しない場合にも AutoPhaseDiffPrompt を選択し、当該プロンプトの "Skip: Cross-file architectural concerns" 指示によりクロスファイル所見が黙って落ちる

findings.title: Ref-file auto-phase prompts omit explicit "read this diff file" directive (diff and arch)
findings.summary: AutoPhaseDiffRefFilePrompt and AutoPhaseArchRefFilePrompt only say "Review the code changes in %s" without telling the agent that %s is a diff file or that it must read it; executeDiffBasedReview does not embed the diff on this path, so reviewers may answer from prompt text alone and miss the actual changes. Independently confirmed by two groups, escalating to blocking.
messages: internal/agent/prompts.go の AutoPhaseDiffRefFilePrompt / AutoPhaseArchRefFilePrompt は冒頭で "Review the code changes in %s" としか述べておらず、対象ファイルを読めという明示指示が無い
internal/agent/prompts.go:188: Ref-file mode no longer tells the agent that `%s` is a diff file or that it must read the file; `executeDiffBasedReview` does not embed the diff on this path
internal/agent/prompts.go:240: Same ref-file regression for arch reviewers: the temp diff path is passed, but the prompt never states that the diff is in that file or instructs the agent to read it

info.title: Reviewer 4: no code-level issues in scoped files (runner.go, tests excluded)
info.summary: Reviewer 4 limited scope to runner.go implementation diff (excluding test files per request) and reported no code-level issues. Note: per cross-check, runner-level RolePrompts propagation was not affirmatively verified.
messages: code-review 相当の読み方で進めます。まずこの差分が実ファイル上でどう接続しているかだけ確認して、指摘できるものがある場合は指定フォーマットに絞って返します
テストファイルは依頼どおり指摘対象から外し、runner.go の実装差分だけを見ています

info.title: Reviewer 2: read-only verification approach, flagged risk area
info.summary: Reviewer 2 used the pasted patch as input (working tree did not show the diff locally) and verified surrounding current code. Flagged the main risk area as whether the new flag reaches the per-agent prompt renderer across all paths — but produced no concrete defect findings of its own.
messages: I'll keep this read-only and focus only on code-level defects visible in the provided files
The working tree doesn't show this diff locally, so I'm using the pasted patch as the review input
The main risk area is whether the new flag actually reaches the per-agent prompt renderer in all affected paths

info.title: Reviewer 3: process notes — verifying RolePrompts wiring and Phase usage
info.summary: Reviewer 3 confirmed RolePrompts wiring exists across CLI, runner, and agent in the working tree, and narrowed scope to Phase-value usage and ref-file prompt behavior before producing the ref-file finding (clustered above as blocking).
messages: レビュー対象の実装経路を実ファイルで確認します。特に resolvePrompts の呼び出し元と RolePrompts が CLI/env/config から ReviewConfig へ渡る境界を見ます
RolePrompts の配線自体はこの作業ツリーでは CLI、runner、agent まで存在しているようです。次に、prompt の実使用時に Phase 値と ref-file prompt がどの形で使われるかを確認

findings.title: Role prompt behavior is only covered by the arch review
findings.summary: The arch review reports that `--role-prompts` is not applied consistently across reviewer execution paths, but no diff group produced a corresponding code-level finding for the cross-file path from CLI/config through runner and agent execution. That leaves the Codex-vs-Claude/Gemini behavior split unvalidated by the file-sliced reviews.

findings.title: Config flag precedence bug was not picked up by the config diff groups
findings.summary: The arch review identifies a CLI/env/config merge precedence bug for `--no-role-prompts=false` under `ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true`, but the diff groups covering `cmd/acr/main.go` and `internal/config/config.go` did not report the issue. This is a cross-boundary gap between CLI flag state and resolved config behavior.

findings.title: Phase-to-prompt responsibility boundary lacks diff-group coverage
findings.summary: The arch review reports that default prompt selection can silently drop cross-file review coverage and that unknown phase values fall through to diff prompts, but the diff groups did not produce matching findings across `diff_review.go`, `prompts.go`, and runner phase assignment. The issue spans prompt rendering and phase orchestration boundaries.

findings.title: Ref-file prompt regression is more severe across arch and diff contexts
findings.summary: Both the arch review and g02 identify that ref-file auto-phase prompts fail to instruct agents to read the diff file. g02 shows the problem affects both diff and arch ref-file prompts, so the advisory architectural concern escalates because it can invalidate review coverage in multiple phases.

findings.title: Codex agent path not covered by any diff group
findings.summary: Arch finding #0 identifies that Codex bypasses resolvePrompts entirely, but no diff group reviewed internal/agent/codex.go — it is absent from g01/g02/g03 target_files. The blocking claim cannot be cross-validated at the diff level, and any future regression in Codex's prompt path would be invisible to diff-level review as currently scoped.

findings.title: g01 missed all blocking issues within its own file scope
findings.summary: g01 reviewed cmd/acr/main.go and internal/agent/diff_review.go and returned only meta/advisory notes. Arch found four blocking defects (#0 Codex bypass site, #1 flag composition, #2 AutoPhaseDiff cross-file suppression, #3 Phase default fall-through) all rooted in those same files. The diff-level group succeeded but produced zero actionable findings on architectural plumbing it had direct visibility into — a coverage gap that weakens the multi-group signal for these files.

findings.title: Ref-file prompt regression independently confirmed across groups
findings.summary: Arch #4 (advisory) and g02 #14 (advisory) independently identify the same regression: AutoPhaseDiffRefFilePrompt / AutoPhaseArchRefFilePrompt no longer instruct the agent that %s is a diff file or that it must be read, so executeDiffBasedReview's ref-file path can produce reviews of the prompt text only. Two independent groups landing on the same defect raises confidence — should be escalated from advisory to blocking since it is a confirmed behavioral regression versus prior ref-file prompts.

findings.title: Runner-level RolePrompts propagation unverified by g03
findings.summary: g03 covered internal/runner/runner.go and reported 'No code-level issues found,' but its meta notes show it explicitly excluded the test files from analysis on request. Combined with arch #0 (Codex path silently no-ops) and arch #2 (AutoPhaseDiff suppression depending on phase), the runner's responsibility for setting RolePrompts on ReviewConfig per-agent / per-phase was not affirmatively verified — only 'nothing jumped out' on the implementation file. Treat as a coverage gap rather than confirmation.

findings.title: Codex Agent Silent RolePrompts Failure
findings.summary: Arch finding (0) identified that Codex agents ignore config.RolePrompts, rendering the feature a no-op for them. Diff group g01, responsible for internal/agent/diff_review.go, failed to identify this cross-agent inconsistency.

findings.title: CLI Flag Precedence Bug Missed
findings.summary: Arch finding (1) identified a flag resolution bug in cmd/acr/main.go where flag state incorrectly overrides env variables when --no-role-prompts=false. Diff group g01 failed to catch this bug.

findings.title: Silent Misclassification in resolvePrompts Missed
findings.summary: Arch findings (2, 3) highlighted that missing or unknown phase values in resolvePrompts default to AutoPhaseDiff, which silently drops cross-file concerns if no arch reviewer exists. Diff group g01, covering internal/agent/diff_review.go, did not identify this structural risk.

findings.title: Ref-File Prompt Read Instruction Missing
findings.summary: Both Arch (4) and Diff group g02 (14) independently identified that prompts in prompts.go fail to instruct the agent to actually read the referenced diff file. This confirmed regression should be escalated to blocking as it risks agents ignoring code changes entirely.
